description:
IB VII, 5. A note (ruqʿa) with a merchant's disposition for his property in contemplation of death. He specifies that his daughter should not marry before the year 500 AH = 1107/08 CE. He then lists various debts owed to him by other people before naming Rabbi Yūsuf (the trustworthy) as his representative for executing on these obligations. The debts are mostly for small amounts. Merchandise mentioned includes eastbound items like a fūṭa and copper going to Yemen, as well as corals (certainly going east) and silk. PG.

Mentioning many people including Abū Yaʿqūb Yūsuf al-Qudsī, Abū ʿImrān b. Avraham al-Kohen, Abū Manṣūr, Abū l-Barakāt al-Ḥalabī (and India trader), Yaḥyā ‘the son of the dyer’ and Abū l-Barakāt b. Shuʿayb.
description_he: מסמך משפטי ביהודית-ערבית. מוחזק בספריית קיימברידג'.
